title: tragacantha marshalliana
intro: Tragacantha marshalliana, also known as Astragalus marshallianus, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, known for its medicinal properties and traditional uses. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. Tragacantha marshalliana is used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, to support respiratory health, boost the immune system, and alleviate symptoms of fatigue and stress.
